mentions.raw_name: Tyrion Lannister (POV; aliases: Hugor Hill, Yollo, Hugor Halfmaester)
Aegon Targaryen (historical, referenced — flew the Black Dread)
The Storm King (historical Westerosi king, referenced)
The tigers (Volantene political faction — Old Blood, pro-military)
The elephants (Volantene political faction — merchants and moneylenders, pro-trade)
Dornish orphans (Greenblood community — Yandry and Ysilla)
Faith of the Seven (Septa Lemore's religion; the Father Above, the Mother, the Father, the Maiden referenced; septa's crystal; seven-colored belt; "mysteries of the Faith")
Summer Islands worship practices (referenced obliquely — "pray in the fashion of the Summer Isles")
Rhoynar (historical river people; Nymeria's civilization; princes rode turtles)
Volantene (history lesson: tigers and elephants, Old Blood)
Dothraki (referenced in history — drove people from their lands east of Qohor)
Ghiscari (Old Ghis tongue; harsh, ugly; Meereenese dialect)
Westerosi / Westermen (Tyrion sounds like a highborn westerman)
Cyvasse set (carved wooden pieces and table; pieces include dragon, trebuchet, light horse, heavy horse, elephant, spears)
Blunted longaxe (referenced as alternative sparring weapon)
Old steel plate armor (Young Griff's sparring gear)
Wooden spoon (Ysilla's weapon against food thieves)
Crossbow (referenced — what Tyrion used to kill Tywin, wants buried with one)
The Fires of the Freehold by Galendro (history of Valyria; no complete copy in Westeros; Citadel's lacks 27 scrolls)
mentions.section: Raw Entity List > In-world Texts & Songs

mentions.raw_name: Dragons, Wyrms, and Wyverns: Their Unnatural History by Septon Barth (ordered destroyed by Baelor the Blessed; Tyrion read a fragment ten years ago)
mentions.section: Raw Entity List > In-world Texts & Songs

mentions.raw_name: Blood and Fire / The Death of Dragons (anonymous, fragmentary, blood-soaked; only surviving copy supposedly in locked vault beneath the Citadel)
mentions.section: Raw Entity List > In-world Texts & Songs

mentions.raw_name: Tyrion's dragonlore manuscript (being written at Griff's command)
mentions.section: Raw Entity List > In-world Texts & Songs

mentions.raw_name: The unnamed dragon-flight quote ("Once a man has seen a dragon in flight, let him stay at home and tend his garden in content, for this wide world has no greater wonder")
mentions.section: Raw Entity List > In-world Texts & Songs

mentions.raw_name: The Old Man of the River (giant horned turtle; treated as a divine/sacred being by Yandry and Ysilla; "Gods and wonders always appear, to attend the birth of kings")
Volantene wars of conquest (after the Doom of Valyria — conquest of Lys and Myr, failed attempt on Tyrosh, defeats that ended tiger dominance)
Battle on Dagger Lake (Qohor and Norvos broke Volantene power with fire galleys)
Dothraki expansion (drove people from settlements east of Qohor)
Triarch (Volantene ruling office — three triarchs: Malaquo, Nyessos, Doniphos)
King's Hand / Hand of the King (Septon Barth held this under Jaehaerys)
Bonesnappers (species of large turtle in the Rhoyne; could bite a man in half)

Turtles of the Rhoyne (many species described: flatbacks, red-ears, softshells, bonesnappers, brown/green/black/clawed/horned turtles; shells with whorls of gold, jade, cream; some large enough to bear a man)
